claims.claim: Testing ten identical lithium cobalt oxide cells revealed a nearly twofold range in self-heating onset temperature (67.4°C to 123.8°C), a full order of magnitude variation in thermal runaway delay time (122 min to 1584 min), and a twofold range in maximum temperature (345.1°C to 726.7°C).
claims.quote: Zhang et al. [5] tested ten identical lithium cobalt oxide cells and found a nearly twofold range of variation in self-heating onset temperature (67.4 ◦C to 123.8 ◦C), a full order of magnitude variation in thermal runaway delay time (122 min to 1584 min), and a similar twofold range in the maximum temperature (345.1 ◦C to 726.7 ◦C).

claims.claim: Repeated component-scale DSC experiments showed heat release standard deviation values of up to 22% of the mean values, and the complete disappearance of certain peaks in repeated experiments.
claims.quote: Lenz et al. [11] repeated component-scale DSC experiments and found numerous sources of uncertainty in the data, from heat release standard deviation values of up to 22% of the mean values (indicating even wider confidence intervals) to the complete disappearance of certain peaks in repeated experiments.

claims.claim: DSC-informed kinetic uncertainty produces 2σ ranges on the initiation and maximum temperatures of 9–17% in ARC cell simulations.
claims.quote: DSC-informed kinetic uncertainty has a significant impact on ARC cell simulation results (for example, 2𝜎 ranges on the initiation and maximum temperatures of 9–17%)

claims.claim: 17% of KDE-sampled ARC simulation cases did not trigger internal short circuit, while 7% triggered significantly delayed internal short circuit, and 76% triggered uncertain but tightly clustered internal short circuit.
claims.quote: 17% of the KDE-sampled cases here did not trigger internal short circuit... 7% of the KDE-sampled cases here triggered internal short circuit late... the 76% of cases that are included in Fig. 6(d), i.e. the cases that triggered uncertain but tightly clustered internal short circuits.

claims.claim: Kinetic models inferred exclusively from DSC data cannot reproduce the low pre-exponential factor (A = 0.035 s⁻¹) required to bridge the gap between SEI decomposition and the onset of remaining exothermic reactions in ARC simulations, because typical DSC-inferred pre-exponential factors range from 10¹⁰ to 10²⁰.
claims.quote: Ref. [66] directly fits the kinetic parameters for this anode reaction to the experimental ARC data (rather than prior DSC data), resulting in a pre-exponential factor here of 𝐴 = 0.035𝑠−1 in the low-temperature range. This is a significant outlier, with typical ranges based on DSC data in the 1010 to 1020 range

claims.claim: The proportions of CO2, CO, and H2 in lithium-ion battery thermal runaway gases are 36.56%, 28.38%, and 22.27%, respectively.
claims.quote: the proportion of CO2, CO, and H2 in thermal runaway gases reaches 36.56 %, 28.38 %, and 22.27 % [6,7], respectively, which is considered as the primary characteristic gas in LIB thermal runaway.

claims.claim: Ti, Mn, and Mo dopants preferentially anchor to the S-surface of the pristine WSSe monolayer, with formation energies of -3.145, -1.282, and -3.776 eV respectively, compared to Se-surface formation energies of -2.022, -0.611, and -1.370 eV.
claims.quote: the doping of metal atoms on the S-surface of WSSe material could make the energy of the system more stable and facilitate the adsorption of gas molecules in the later work.

claims.claim: TM doping reduces the bandgap of WSSe from 2.041 eV to 0.295 eV (Ti), 0.795 eV (Mn), and 0.212 eV (Mo), with Mo–WSSe showing the largest reduction of 1.829 eV.
claims.quote: The band gaps of Ti–WSSe, Mn–WSSe and Mo–WSSe monolayers are 0.295, 0.795 and 0.212 eV, respectively. Compared with the pristine WSSe monolayers with a bandgap of 2.041 eV, that of TM-WSSe was reduced to some extent, and the Mo–WSSe was the smallest with a reduction of 1.829 eV.

claims.claim: The adsorption energy of TM-WSSe systems is nearly 7 times that of pristine WSSe, indicating that TM atom doping significantly enhances adsorption performance.
claims.quote: Among the remaining seven chemical adsorption systems, the adsorption energy are almost 7 times higher than intrinsic WSSe.

claims.claim: Recovery time exhibits a negative correlation with temperature, and higher adsorption energy causes a prolonged desorption process.
claims.quote: The recovery time exhibits a negative correlation with temperature, and higher adsorption energy causes a prolonged desorption process.

claims.claim: The electrochemical impedance of lithium-ion batteries first decreases and then increases with increasing temperature, with a change of 3–4 orders of magnitude before thermal runaway.
claims.quote: the electrochemical impedance of LIBs changes by 3–4 orders of magnitude before TR

claims.claim: As temperature rises in a lithium-ion battery, micropore closure in the separator and electrolyte vaporization between electrodes lead to an increase in resistance R and capacitance C, causing impedance to increase.
claims.quote: as the temperature further rises, the micropores in the separator close, and vaporization of the electrolyte occurs between the electrodes, which leads to an increase in resistance R and capacitance C

claims.claim: The phase angle h of cell A at approximately 1 kHz has the lowest sensitivity to SOC, with a range of 1.97° and a variance of 0.39, while its fluctuation range across different temperatures is more than three times that of the SOC sensitivity analysis, and the variance is more than seventeen times higher.
claims.quote: the sensitivity of h to SOC is lowest around 1 kHz, with a range of 1.97° and a variance of 0.39. The fluctuation range of h in different temperatures is more than three times of that in the sensitivity analysis of SOC, and the variance is more than seventeen times

claims.claim: At a cell temperature of 45°C, the impedance drop value of cell A is approximately 2 mΩ, which equals half of the maximum impedance change range (|Z|max − |Z|min) at the specific SOC, and this threshold was selected as the first-stage thermal runaway warning indicator.
claims.quote: The temperature of the cell is 45 °C when the impedance drop value is approximately 2 mΩ, which is 1/2 of (|Z|max−|Z|min) at the specific SOC

claims.claim: In a parallel module, the voltage drop of an individual cell undergoing thermal runaway is not reflected in the module voltage, but the impedance of the failing cell increases abruptly, and this impedance increase can be used as a stage-III thermal runaway warning indicator.
claims.quote: The voltage drop of the individual cells will occur after the cell self-heating occurs, but due to the properties of the parallel module, the voltage drop will not be reflected in the module voltage. At this point, the impedance of the cell increased abruptly, in this case by 2.2 mΩ

claims.claim: In a parallel module experiment, the stage-I thermal runaway warning was triggered at a cell temperature of 50.1°C and the stage-II warning at 94.2°C, with advance warning times of 307 s and 181 s before thermal runaway, respectively.
claims.quote: the temperature of the cell was measured to be 50.1 °C... the temperature of the cell was 94.2 °C... Stage-I 307 [s]... Stage-II 181 [s]
